details: The "Shramashakthi Special Women's Scheme" was launched by the Minorities Welfare Department, Government of Karnataka, and implemented by the Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation. This is a special scheme for the economic empowerment of widowed, divorced, unmarried women of the minority community. Under this scheme, ₹25,000/- loan and ₹25,000/- subsidy will be provided to undertake various economic activities.
benefits: A total of ₹50,000/- is provided, comprising ₹25,000/- as a loan and ₹25,000/- as a subsidy.
eligibility: The applicant should be a female.
The applicant should be a widowed, divorced, unmarried woman.
The applicant should belong to a religious minority community as defined in the Government Order.
The applicant should be a permanent resident of Karnataka.
The applicant's age should be between 18 to 55 years.
The annual family income of the applicant from all sources should be below ₹3,50,000/-.
The applicant's family member should not be a State/Central Government employee.
The applicant or family members should not have availed of benefits under any other scheme (Excluding the Arivu Scheme) of the KMDCL in the last 5 years.

online: Step-01: Applicant need to visits the Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation
Step-03: Enter "Aadhaar Number" Fill in the "Captcha" and Select "Next".
Step-04: Applicant will get OTP in his/her Aadhaar Linked Mobile Number.
Step-05: Enter "OTP" and select "Continue", After that Allow the official notice.
Step-06: After verifying the Aadhaar again fill in the "OTP" and Submit.
Step-07: Select the scheme "Sikhligar’s Community Development Scheme" and fill in the basic details father's name, gender, qualification detail
Step-08: Select "Next" Fill in the address detail and again select "Next".
Step-09: Upload the required document passport-size photo, educational document, income certificate, etc.
Step-10: Click "Proceed" and Select "Submit Application" and get an Application ID.

documents_required: Caste/Minority Certificate (issued by the competent authority).
Income Certificate (issued by the competent authority).
Copy of Aadhaar as Residential Proof.

faqs.question: What is this scheme about?
faqs.answer: This scheme provides financial assistance to minority women for economic activities.

faqs.question: Who can apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Widowed, divorced, and unmarried minority women aged 18-55 from Karnataka.

faqs.question: What is the financial assistance provided?
What documents are needed for the application?
faqs.answer: Project report, caste/minority certificate, income certificate, Aadhaar card, bank passbook, and self-declaration forms.

faqs.question: How do I apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Applications can be submitted at KMDCL's official website online.

faqs.question: Can I apply if I work for the government?
faqs.answer: No, applicants or their family members should not be government employees.

faqs.question: Can I reapply if I received benefits from KMDCL before?
faqs.answer: No, you cannot apply if you have availed benefits from KMDCL in the last 5 years, except for the Arivu Education Loan Scheme.

faqs.question: Is a guarantor required for the loan?
faqs.answer: Yes, a surety is needed for the loan application.

faqs.question: What is the purpose of the subsidy?
faqs.answer: The subsidy helps reduce the financial burden of starting an economic activity.

faqs.question: Is there any application fee?
faqs.answer: No, recently no mention of any fee for more information check with KMDCL website.
